$comment: Single source of truth for the coordinated release. The npm packages all publish at `version` (sync mode — see RELEASING.md). The model identity (which trained artifact + tokenizer the neural-weights-* packages bundle) lives under `weights`, decoupled from the artifact's historical filename. Paths are relative to `weights.dataRoot` (override the root with MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT, or the individual files with MAILWOMAN_PUBLISH_MODEL / MAILWOMAN_PUBLISH_TOKENIZER). Bump `version` here in lockstep with the release; copy-weights.ts, the ship script, and the model card all read this instead of hardcoding the numbers.

weights.lineage: #884 vocab-splice + FVT mean-init on the UNCHANGED v1.9.3a3-anchor-absorption encoder (v4.15.0, step 80000) — the training-free CZ/PL/SK/SI diacritic fix. The 48k unigram vocab had diacritic chars but no diacritic subwords, so CZ/PL names fragmented (~3.3× fertility) and resolved to the wrong city; a shard retrain (v196) was falsified (CZ wrong-city 44→58% at convergence). Fix: splice ONLY diacritic-bearing pieces from a CZ/PL SP unigram into the vocab (48,000→58,582 — English byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION, asserted 0/2000) + mean-init the 10,582 new embedding rows from constituents; zero gradient steps (the 2k fine-tune ablation was retired as slightly harmful). COORDINATED bump: the spliced tokenizer ships WITH the model. Gates (pre-registered, n=1k reservoir OA sets, int8 ship artifact): US-2k row dump BYTE-IDENTICAL to v4.15.0; wrong-city CZ 22.4→14.8%, PL 27.9→7.9%, SK 22.2→13.2%, SI 18.9→10.6% (SK/SI free-ride the CZ/PL pieces, zero added bytes); p50 improve CIs wholly negative ×4. Cost (operator-accepted): int8 29.8→33.8 MB (+4.06 MB embedding bytes, ~3.8 MB over the ~30 MB browser SLO); WASM latency flat. Pre-splice baseline: parity-scorecard-2026-07-02.md (17/17). int8 md5 12fa2d645162e90cf028dbc333218f2c; tokenizer md5 b0397ce3d89ab459be6dd901eae8f0a1.

softFeed.$comment: Soft-feed artifacts (#718 D1) materialized into each neural-weights-<locale> workspace at publish time by copy-weights.ts so the library default `loadFromWeights` feeds the channels the anchor+gazetteer-trained model expects (closing the anchor-OFF crater). The PCB1 postcode binary is built from the WOF postcode shard (per locale's country code); the gazetteer lexicon is the codex-generated repo file. Paths under `db` are relative to weights.dataRoot/wof. Like the weights binaries, these are skipped in CI (MAILWOMAN_SKIP_WEIGHTS_COPY) — the publish workflow places them via the HF fetch step.

assets.$comment: Where the model release lives for the demo + the CI weights pull. The release path embeds `version`; publish.yml derives the same path from the model card so CI and local stay in sync.
